articles.title: Who Is Sriram Krishnan? Donald Trump’s AI Advisor To Quit Key White House Post
articles.description: Sriram Krishnan will step down as a senior White House AI policy adviser at the end of June. His departure comes as the Trump administration advances new artificial intelligence policies and oversight measures.

articles.title: China’s gig economy is an employment reservoir - but is the well drying up?
articles.description: More than 200 million people in China are flexibly employed, but AI adoption, unstable income and poor social security make positions precarious.
articles.content:
The air carried a chill in Hengdian, one of China’s best-known hubs for drama and film production. Yu Shutian, a 30-year-old actress, was on her way to set at 3.30am, donned in a floral shirt and darkened make-up to play a rural villain.

articles.title: Commentary: What if AI retraining is just a comforting lie?
articles.description: If upskilling is being touted as the bridge to an AI future, it must lead somewhere, says Catherine Thorbecke for Bloomberg Opinion.
articles.content: TOKYO: No one knows whether artificial intelligence will trigger a white-collar jobpocalypse. The loudest warnings still come from people building and selling the technology, whose predictions often double as hype-mongering or cover for unrelated cos... [5562 chars]

articles.title: Quote of the day by Nvidia CEO, Jensen Huang: "I appreciate that many of us grew up and enjoyed science fiction, but it's not helpful" — on quantifying the existential risks posed by AI
articles.description: With prominent scientists and technologists frequently warning of the risks of AI, Jensen Huang eases concerns by lamenting the influence of sci-fi
articles.content: One of the most important figures at the heart of the rise of AI is Jensen Huang, co-founder and CEO of Nvidia. His company manufactures the GPUs that excel in training and inference, and is positioning itself at the heart of the infrastructure and e... [1329 chars]

articles.title: Trump marks D-Day with AI video of him riding a lion and photo portraying Obama library as trash can
articles.description: Meanwhile, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth warned European allies of being stormed by ‘different, dangerous ideologies’

articles.title: Scammers Are Weaponizing AI For Their Nefarious Goals As Fraud-Related Losses For U.S. Consumers Approach $1 Billion, With Little Respite
articles.description: Using elaborate scams using AI is getting out of hand, as a new report states that U.S. customers are losing close to $1 billion
articles.content: The AI boom isn’t just exacerbating a RAM and storage crisis, but its inception has allowed scammers a whole new layer of creativity to deprive innocent Americans of their hard-earned money. According to the latest statistics, AI-fueled scams are app... [3007 chars]

articles.description: "Nationalization of AI will accelerate the corporate-government fusion we’re already sliding toward," wrote Sacks.

articles.title: Cathie Wood Ditches Fed Rate Hike Fears With Bullish Market Note
articles.description: Cathie Wood says markets are misreading strong jobs data, arguing AI-driven productivity growth could lower inflation and ease Fed rate fears.
articles.content:
Cathie Wood took to social media to ease investor worries of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates. Her comments follow a stronger-than-expected U.S. jobs report.

articles.title: Public ownership in AI: Trump and Sanders find common ground
articles.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has met with Sen. Bernie Sanders to discuss public ownership in AI companies, and President Trump too has expressed interest in the idea.
articles.content:
It was perhaps a surprising private overture from OpenAI Chief Executive Sam Altman to Sen. Bernie Sanders.
